summary: High‑energy Easter Sunday show with killer Tons Of Steel & Black Muddy River
blurb: The first set shines with tight jams on Cassidy and the seamless China > Rider transition, while the second set delivers powerful vocal numbers such as Tons Of Steel and an unforgettable Black Muddy River finale. Jerry’s guitar is on fire throughout and Brent’s soulful singing adds extra sparkle.
review:
On Easter Sunday, April 19, 1987, the Dead took Irvine Meadows by storm. The night opened with a breezy Mississippi Half‑Step that rolled straight into Franklin's Tower before launching into a first set that many reviewers call the high‑point of the evening—tight jams on Cassidy and a flawless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue that kept the crowd in a perpetual groove. Jerry Garcia was “on” all night, delivering his signature soaring tone on Black Muddy River and ripping through Tons Of Steel with a joyous, almost celebratory edge. Brent Mydland’s soulful vocals shone on both Tons Of Steel and Man Smart, Woman Smarter, while Phil Lesh anchored the band with solid, emotive bass work (notably on Box of Rain). The rhythm section—Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann—provided a hot, percussive drive, highlighted by an epic pre‑drums Cumberland Blues jam that set up a spacey second‑set adventure through Space, The Other One, and a soaring Wharf Rat. The show closed with the rare encore combination of Not Fade Away and Brokedown Palace, leaving fans with one of the most memorable Easter Sunday experiences of the late ’80s.

Across all available sources the sound quality is superb: the Clugston SBD (gd87-04-19.sbd.clugston.11883.sbeok.shnf) captures every nuance with crystal‑clear balance, making it the definitive listening source for this legendary night.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Five recordings are clearly positive and one is mixed; no negative consensus appears. Reviewers repeatedly praise the first‑set jams, Jerry’s guitar on Black Muddy River, and Brent’s vocal work, yielding a strong positive sentiment across the board.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture—crisp, balanced mix with no dropouts; showcases the high‑energy performance and highlights like Tons Of Steel, Cassidy, China>Rider, and Black Muddy River.

key_highlights: First set jams on Cassidy and seamless China > Rider transition
Jerry’s soaring solo on Black Muddy River
Brent’s soulful vocal delivery on Tons Of Steel and Man Smart, Woman Smarter
Epic pre‑drums Cumberland Blues jam that fuels the Space segment

band_performance.Jerry: On fire all night—joyful guitar tone, soaring solo on Black Muddy River, and tight leads throughout.
band_performance.Phil: Solid bass foundation with emotive singing on Box of Rain; overall rhythm section stayed high‑energy despite a few noted “lagging” arrangement moments.
band_performance.Brent: Soulful vocals on Tons Of Steel, Man Smart, Woman Smarter and Black Muddy River add extra sparkle to the set.
band_performance.Mickey: Percussive energy shines—hot pre‑drums Cumberland Blues jam and overall epic drumming throughout the show.
band_performance.Billy: Drum work tight and powerful; drives the high‑energy first set and fuels the spacey second‑set explorations.
